They can score a maximum of M1A0 M1A1M1A0 if they use an incorrect equation such as a + b = 1. M1 Attempts to substitute 1пӮұ into f(x) and set equal to 4 to obtain an equation in a and b. Powers of вҲ’1 need not be seen, accept e.g. 1 вҖ“ a вҖ“ 3 вҖ“ b + 5 = 4. Condone invisible brackets on powers for the M mark, but penalise in the A (if incorrect). Alternatively, attempts long division reaching a remainder which is then set equal to 4. Look for a quotient starting ..x+ and linear remainder in a and b. It may be seen in tabulated form. Another alternative method: Writes () ( ) (1)4fxxxxxпҒЎ пҒў пҒ§=+++++ (allow with Оі = 1), expands and equates coefficients, and proceeds to eliminate Оұ, ОІ and Оі from the equations. FYI by long division the quotient is (1)(2)2xaxax a b+ вҲ’ + вҲ’ вҲ’ + + + and the remainder is 3abвҲ’вҲ’ . By equating coefficients look for 45пҒ§+= (may be implied), 1aпҒЎ=+ , 1bпҒў=+ , 3пҒЎпҒўвҲ’=+ being solved correctly. (b) M1 Attempts to substitute 2пӮұ into f(x) and set equal to 23пӮұ to obtain an equation in a and b. Again, accept attempts at long division leading to a remainder linear in a and b (which is 829ab++ ), which is then set equal to 23пӮұ , or via equating coefficients (look for at least three equations formed). May have substituted for a or b already. A1 8232 oe ab+ = вҲ’ need not be fully gathered but powers should be evaluated. So e.g. accept 16 812 2523ab+ вҲ’ + + = вҲ’ or accept 16 812 2"(1)" 523aa+ вҲ’ + вҲ’ вҲ’ + = вҲ’ if substitution for b happens first (similar for a). Via equating coefficients, all equations should be correct. dM1 A

Alt 2: (Contradiction type proof) M1 Starts with the negation of the statement and gathers terms on ones side of the equation.
